commit.message:
feat: support multiple coverage formats for monorepos

Format input now accepts comma-separated values (e.g., "gocover,lcov,cobertura")
for monorepos with multiple languages. With auto-discovery, each format finds
its own files independently. With explicit paths, files are automatically routed
to the correct parser via try-all-parsers fallback.

Adds monorepo integration test (Go + Node + Python) to CI.

Add end-to-end tests using Playwright to complement existing Vitest unit tests.

Setup:
- Add @playwright/test as dev dependency
- Configure playwright.config.ts with webServer, base URL, and multi-browser targets
- Add pnpm test:e2e script
- Add e2e CI job to GitHub Actions workflow
- Update .gitignore and biome.json for Playwright artifacts

Test coverage:
- Character creation: fill form → character appears in list
- Character sheet view: navigate to character → all sections render
- Character editing: modify ability scores → changes persist after refresh
- Character deletion: delete character → removed from list
- Dark mode toggle: switch theme → verify CSS variables change
- Responsive layout: key pages render at mobile breakpoints

Shared test fixtures provide common setup for page navigation and character creation.

Closes #24

commit.message:
refactor(plugin,macro): reduce cyclomatic complexity of plugin, macro, setup, selfupdate, and rules packages (#267)

Extract helper functions to reduce gocyclo complexity below threshold 15:

- plugin/engine.go: parseHookResult (19→11) split into parseActionFromDict, parseDataFromDict, parseResponseFromDict
- plugin/httpconv.go: ApplyHTTPRequestChanges (17→7) split into applyMethod, applyURL, applyHost, applyRequestHeaders, extractBody
- macro/engine.go: validateMacro (21→9) split into validateStep, validateStepErrorPolicy, validateStepGuard, validateExtractRules
- macro/engine.go: executeStep (16→8) split into resolveStepParams, executeWithRetry, handleStepFailure
- setup/playwright.go: WritePlaywrightConfig (21→11) split into readExistingConfig, unmarshalJSONSection, applyProxySettings, applyHTTPSOption, writeConfig
- selfupdate/updater.go: Upgrade (17→7) split into downloadAndReplace, resolveExecPath, downloadChecksums, downloadVerifyAndInstall
- proxy/rules/modification.go: compileRule (16→8) split into compileConditionPatterns, compileBodyPattern, compileRegexWithLimit

Refs: USK-260

commit.message:
feat: implement B/C/D — context aggregation, hot reload sync, eBPF map update

B — Slow Path context aggregation:
  - New slow_path/context.rs: ContextWindow accumulates recent event vectors
    per PID within a configurable time window (time_window_secs from config).
    On each event, the current vector is blended (α=0.7) with the PID's
    recency-weighted behavioral history before Qdrant search — reduces false
    positives from isolated one-off events and gives the anomaly detector
    richer behavioral signal. 4 unit tests included.
  - slow_path/mod.rs: integrates ContextWindow with Mutex for interior
    mutability; context vector is read before the current event is pushed
    so the event does not pollute its own search baseline.

C — Hot reload sync:
  - hotreload.rs now accepts a watch::Sender<Config> and broadcasts the
    new config on every successful reload — pipeline components rebuild
    without restarting the daemon.
  - main.rs: creates watch::channel, passes Sender to hotreload task and
    Receiver to run_pipeline. Pipeline uses tokio::select! to interleave
    event processing with config-change notifications, then rebuilds
    ScopeFilter, FastPath, and SlowPath in-place.

D — eBPF map offloading on hot reload:
  - Shared Arc<Mutex<Option<Enforcer>>> accessible from both the collector
    task (initializer) and run_pipeline (updater).
  - On every hot-reload config change, run_pipeline calls
    enforcer.load_rules(fast_path.rules()) to repopulate BLOCKED_COMMS /
    BLOCKED_PORTS / BLOCKED_UIDS in the kernel — new block rules take
    effect immediately without a process restart.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Extend parallax correction from 11-param to 24-param: 15p/18p/24p per-planet tiers with LOOCV

Add heliocentric distance terms (L-O: 1/s, sin(u)/d², sin(2u)/s, cos(u)/s),
time-modulated 2nd harmonics (P-R), and extended terms (S-Y: T/d, cos(u)/d²,
1/s², sin(u)/s², cos(3u)/s, sin(3u)/s). Each planet uses the tier with lowest
cross-validated error: Mercury/Mars use 24p, Saturn/Neptune use 18p, others 15p.

Results: Mercury 0.48°→0.03°, Mars 0.31°→0.04°, all 7 planets under 0.09°.
Includes greedy forward selection and candidate search tools for basis function
discovery. Documentation fully rewritten.

fix: use case-insensitive match for CalendarEvent status update

The CalendarEvent table stores session_type values from OpenF1 with
original casing (e.g., "Race", "Qualifying", "Practice 1"), but
ingest_session() callers often pass lowercase values like "race".

The exact filter_by() match was failing to find the calendar event,
leaving status as "scheduled" even after successful data ingestion.

- gmx: batch equity curve + stats writes (10 concurrent) instead of

commit.message:
feat: dramatically improve vocaloid synthesis for human-like vocals

- Replace sawtooth with LF-model glottal pulse (PeriodicWave, 64 harmonics,
  12dB/octave spectral tilt, per-harmonic jitter)
- Update to Peterson & Barney (1952) male voice formant frequencies
- Add jitter (~0.8% pitch variation) and shimmer (~3% amplitude variation)
- Add breath noise between vocal phrases (lowpass filtered)
- Delay vibrato onset 200ms after note start (natural singing behavior)
- Add glottal attack transients (brief noise burst at syllable onset)
- Add aspiration blend (highpass noise mixed with carrier for breathiness)
- Smooth formant interpolation between vowels (40ms coarticulation)
- Prosody contours with phrase-level rise/fall
- Add subharmonics for power tracks (battle, epic styles)
- Same improvements applied to Vocal Lab tool

Add in-game Map Editor and Map Selector

Map Selector (Play button → modal):
- Lists Default Map + all user-created maps
- Each row: name, size, Play / Edit / Delete buttons
- Active map marked with ★
- Plays default or any saved custom map

Map Editor (Map Editor button → full-screen overlay):
- Left-click paint, right-click erase tiles
- Tile types: Wall, Floor, Pillar, Spawn, Door
- P key or palette button: place player start point
- T key or palette button: toggle torches
- Scroll to zoom, middle-mouse or Space+drag to pan
- Keys 1-5 for quick tile select
- Save: stores map in localStorage, appears in selector
- Play: saves and immediately starts the game on that map
- Load Default: pre-fills editor with the hardcoded map
- New Map / Clear All buttons

Storage: localStorage 'deadsurge_maps' array, 'deadsurge_active' key
Game startup auto-loads active map; reset to default on delete

Remove --no-background, promote --no-progressive, carry foreground through (#1381)

Removes the deprecated `--no-background` flag (replaced by
`--foreground`) from
`remove`, `hook post-start`, `hook post-switch`, and `hook post-remove`.
Removes
all supporting deprecation infrastructure: warning message,
`is_foreground_mode`
helper, `DEPRECATED_ARGS` completion filtering.

Promotes `--no-progressive` from deprecated to hidden flag with inline
mention
in `--progressive`'s help text, using `flag_pair()` for consistent
handling.

Carries `foreground: bool` through the entire call chain instead of
negating to
`background` at call sites. Refactors `print_message` to use
`success_message()`/`progress_message()` helpers instead of manual color
construction, adding `FormattedMessage::append()` for integration
symbols that
need native styling outside the message's color span.

> _This was written by Claude Code on behalf of maximilian_

commit.message:
Fix conditional parsing, TUP_CWD restoration, and unknown line handling

1. Conditional errors: else/endif outside if blocks now produce proper
   parse errors. Unclosed if blocks detected at EOF ("missing endif").
   Max nesting depth of 8 enforced. Fixes t2079, t2080, t2082.

2. Unknown line handling: Unrecognized lines in inactive if/else branches
   are silently ignored (matching C tup). In active branches they produce
   errors. This allows constructs like `endif #comment` in dead code.

3. TUP_CWD restoration: After include/include_rules returns, TUP_CWD is
   restored to the current file's value. Previously, the included file's
   TUP_CWD persisted and corrupted subsequent variable uses. Fixes t2085.

Test results: t0000-t2179 now 55/194 passing (up from 51).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add solo_source_credibility_threshold to config dataclass and loader (#3)

* feat: add solo_source_credibility_threshold to Config dataclass and YAML

Add `solo_source_credibility_threshold: float = 0.85` to the Config
dataclass (after minimum_sources_required) and wire it through
load_config() so the value is honoured when set in config.yaml.
Also add the corresponding entry to config.yaml with the default 0.85.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add solo_source_credibility_threshold to topics settings

- _load_topics_settings() now includes solo_source_credibility_threshold
  (float, default 0.85) alongside the existing settings
- POST /topics/settings validates the new field as a float in [0.0, 1.0]
  and persists it to config.yaml next to minimum_sources_required

  the settings grid, following the same pattern as the existing inputs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add solo high-credibility path to verify_cluster()

Add a `solo_threshold` parameter (default 0.85) so a single source
with credibility_weight >= solo_threshold can pass verification on its
own. The solo path uses `credibility_weight * 80` as its confidence
formula, deliberately capping it below the multi-source ceiling so
callers can distinguish solo-source results. The existing multi-source
formula (avg_credibility * 60 + source_bonus) is completely unchanged.

Update test_duplicate_source_counts_once to reflect the new behaviour
and add test_single_source_below_solo_threshold_fails to cover the
below-threshold case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: wire solo_threshold config into verify_cluster caller

Pass solo_source_credibility_threshold from the loaded Config object
through to the verify_cluster() call in scheduler.py, so the
configurable threshold is respected at runtime instead of falling back
to the hardcoded default.

Add one-time scripts for mega-cluster splits and embedding backfill

split-mega-clusters.ts: breaks oversized clusters using agglomerative
re-clustering with centroid linkage at 0.72 threshold. Articles without
embeddings become singletons. Supports --dry-run and --threshold flags.

backfill-embeddings.ts: generates embeddings for articles missing them
using text-embedding-3-small. Rate-limited at ~3/sec with progress
logging. Supports --limit, --days, and --dry-run flags.

Both scripts have already been run against production:
- 9 mega-clusters split into 468 sub-clusters + 131 singletons
- 1,537 articles backfilled to 100% embedding coverage (7d)

commit.message:
Rewrite clustering pipeline to use centroid matching with size caps

Replace member-to-member similarity matching with centroid-based
clustering across all ingestion paths (ingest, discover, discover-web,
cluster-maintenance). This prevents semantic drift that caused
mega-clusters mixing unrelated stories.

Key changes:
- All clustering queries compare against AVG(embedding) centroids
- Enforce MAX_CLUSTER_SIZE (25) via HAVING COUNT(*) < cap
- Raise similarity threshold from 0.6 to 0.68 (SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD)
- Raise merge threshold from 0.55 to 0.72 (MERGE_THRESHOLD)
- Add dedup checks before embedding generation to save API costs
- Add ON CONFLICT (canonical_url) DO NOTHING to discover-web inserts
- Remove unused opts.queries param from discover.ts

commit.message:
fix(pages): wrangler auth check hangs on headless servers

wrangler whoami can hang indefinitely on headless/SSH servers when
trying to reach the Cloudflare API, causing bv --pages to always
report "wrangler not authenticated" even when valid credentials exist.

Fix: check CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN env var first, then check the wrangler
config file (~/.config/.wrangler/config/default.toml) directly for a
valid oauth_token. Only fall back to wrangler whoami (with a 10s
timeout) if neither fast check succeeds.

commit.message:
Add contact page and improve navigation responsiveness

- Add new contact page with split layout, random photo, and email/Instagram links
- Update navigation across all pages to include Contact link
- Make header navigation responsive with fluid font sizes and gaps
- Remove "Get in Touch" section from about page (now on contact page)
- Fix moment page header overlap and add scroll support for small viewports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(crosspost): rewrite social prompts for discussion over promotion

- Replace marketing-oriented prompts with discussion/insight focus
- Rename "announcement" style to "insight", rewrite all style descriptions
- Remove mandatory blog links, CTAs, and promotional language
- Add explicit anti-promotion rules ("never say check out, learn more")
- Change Reddit channels from naia to luke (developer) style
- Update subreddit angles to focus on community value, not project pitch

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
